---
title: "Replication_How Changes in Financial Incentives Affect the Duration of Unemployment"
author: "Jan, Rito, Gian"
format: pdf
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
rm(list = ls())
library(foreign)  ## for: read.dta
## do not use haven::read_dta since this messes up `udat$type` factor labels
library(tidyverse)

library(survival)
#setwd()
udat <- read.dta("fi.dta")  
udat <- udat[,1:134] ## get rid of some superfluous variables
udat <- as_tibble(udat)

dim(udat)  ## N = 225,821
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
glimpse(udat[,1:36])

```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

```{r}
table(udat$type)
```

```{r}
## Computation of average spells when durations are truncated at 104 weeks
udat <- udat %>%
  mutate(dur104 = dur,
        dur104 = ifelse(dur104 > 104, 104, dur104)) 


```

Diff-in-Diff estimates

```{r}
# Raw means by type and period
table4 <- udat %>%
  group_by(type, after) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = round(mean(dur104), 2),
    sd   = round(sd(dur104) / sqrt(n()), 2),   # SE
    N    = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Pivot to wide: columns 0 (before) and 1 (after)
table4_wide <- table4 %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(mean, sd, N), names_to = "name") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = after, values_from = value)

# diff and DiD (mean rows only; control DiD = 0 by construction)
control_diff <- table4_wide %>%
  filter(type == "control", name == "mean") %>%
  mutate(diff = `1` - `0`) %>%
  pull(diff)

table4_wide <- table4_wide %>%
  mutate(
    diff = if_else(name == "mean", round(`1` - `0`, 2),        NA_real_),
    DiD  = if_else(name == "mean", round(diff - control_diff, 2), NA_real_)
  )

print(table4_wide)

```

```{r}
# Model: dur104 ~ type + after  (control is reference category)
# coef[2:3] = typePBD and typeRR dummies

# Set control as reference category
udat <- udat %>%
  mutate(type = relevel(factor(type), ref = "control"))

# DiD regression
mod1 <- lm(dur104 ~ type * after, data = udat)


broom::tidy(mod1) %>%
  filter(str_detect(term, ":")) %>%
  select(term, estimate, std.error)


```

Survival Function A typical Kaplan-Meier survivor function:

```{r}

# Fit KM separately for each group × period combination
km_fit <- survfit(Surv(dur104, uncc) ~ type + after, data = udat)

summary(km_fit)

```

Plot the K-M estimates

```{r}
###Survival Function
library(ggplot2)

# Extract KM estimates into a tidy dataframe
km_df <- broom::tidy(km_fit)

# Plot: one panel per type, before vs after
km_df %>%
  mutate(
    after = if_else(str_detect(strata, "after=1"), "After", "Before"),
    type  = str_extract(strata, "(?<=type=)[^,]+")
  ) %>%
  filter(time <= 100) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = time, y = estimate, linetype = after)) +
  geom_step() +
  facet_wrap(~ type, nrow = 2) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Before" = "dashed", "After" = "solid")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20)) +
  labs(x = "Unemployment duration (weeks)",
       y = "Survival probability",
       linetype = NULL) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r}
### Unemployment Hazard Rates (Figure 4)
km_df %>%
  mutate(
    after    = if_else(str_detect(strata, "after=1"), "After", "Before"),
    type     = str_extract(strata, "(?<=type=)[^,]+"),
    type = factor(type, levels = c("PBD", "RR", "PBD and RR", "control")),
    time_bin = floor(time / 4) * 4
  ) %>%
  filter(time <= 100) %>%
  group_by(type, after, time_bin) %>%
  summarise(
    n.event = sum(n.event),
    n.risk  = first(n.risk),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(hazard = n.event / n.risk) %>%
  group_by(type, after) %>%
  mutate(
    hazard_smooth = locpoly(
      x         = time_bin,
      y         = hazard,
      degree    = 3,
      bandwidth = 8,
      gridsize  = n()
    )$y
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = time_bin, y = hazard_smooth, linetype = after)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~ type, nrow = 2) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Before" = "dashed", "After" = "solid")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA)) +
  labs(
    x        = "Unemployment duration (weeks)",
    y        = "Exit hazard",
    linetype = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```




Piecewise Exponential Likelihood
```{r}
# ── Prepare treatment indicators 
udat <- udat %>%
  mutate(
    type = relevel(factor(type), ref = "control"),
    all  = tr * (t39 + t52)
  )

# ── Person-period dataset 
breaks <- seq(from = 3, to = 59, by = 4)
labels <- paste("(", c(0, breaks), ",", c(breaks, 104), "]", sep = "")

gux <- survSplit(Surv(dur104, uncc) ~., data = udat, cut = breaks,
                 end = "time", event = "death", start = "start", episode = "interval")

gux <- gux %>%
  mutate(
    exposure = time - start,
    interval = factor(interval + 1, labels = labels)
  )

# ── Piecewise Constant PH Model (equation 2 in Lalive et al.) 
ph_model <- glm(
  death ~   interval                  # baseline hazard λ_0l
          + t39_tr                    # β_1l: ex ante PBD 30→39 vs control
          + t52_tr                    # β_2l: ex ante PBD 30→52 vs control
          + tr                        # β_3l: ex ante RR vs control
          + all                       # β_4l: ex ante PBD+RR group
          + after                     # β_5l: common time trend (A89)
          + tr_a0  + tr_a1  + tr_a2  + tr_a3  + tr_a4        # δ_3l: RR effect per interval
          + tr_a5  + tr_a6  + tr_a7  + tr_a8  + tr_a9
          + tr_a10 + tr_a11 + tr_a12 + tr_a13 + tr_a14 + tr_a15
          + t39tra0  + t39tra1  + t39tra2  + t39tra3  + t39tra4  # δ_1l: PBD39 per interval
          + t39tra5  + t39tra6  + t39tra7  + t39tra8  + t39tra9
          + t39tra10 + t39tra11 + t39tra12 + t39tra13 + t39tra14 + t39tra15
          + t52tra0  + t52tra1  + t52tra2  + t52tra3  + t52tra4  # δ_2l: PBD52 per interval
          + t52tra5  + t52tra6  + t52tra7  + t52tra8  + t52tra9
          + t52tra10 + t52tra11 + t52tra12 + t52tra13 + t52tra14 + t52tra15
          - 1
          + offset(log(exposure)),
  family = poisson(link = "log"),
  data   = gux %>% filter(exposure > 0)
)

summary(ph_model)

# Extract treatment effects
broom::tidy(ph_model) %>%
  filter(str_detect(term, "tra|tr_a")) %>%
  select(term, estimate, std.error, p.value) %>%
  mutate(
    hazard_ratio = exp(estimate),
    ci_low       = exp(estimate - 1.96 * std.error),
    ci_high      = exp(estimate + 1.96 * std.error)
  ) %>%
  print(n = Inf)

```
